SUM.summary: Sum together the numbers. Equivalent to number1 + number2 + ...

AVERAGE.summary: Returns the average of the numbers.

MAX.summary: Returns the largest of the given numbers.

MIN.summary: Returns the smallest of the given numbers.

ROUND.summary: Rounds the valor to the number of decimal places given by "precision" (Specifically, ROUND will round to the nearest integer at the specified precision, with ties broken by rounding half up toward positive infinity.)

ROUNDUP.summary: Rounds the valor to the number of decimal places given by "precision" always rounding up, i.e., away from zero. (You must give a valor for the precision or the function will not work.)

ROUNDDOWN.summary: Rounds the valor to the number of decimal places given by "precision" always rounding down, i.e., toward zero. (You must give a valor for the precision or the function will not work.)

CEILING.summary: Returns the nearest integer multiple of significance that is greater than or equal to the valor. If no significance is provided, a significance of 1 is assumed.

FLOOR.summary: Returns the nearest integer multiple of significance that is less than or equal to the valor. If no significance is provided, a significance of 1 is assumed.

EVEN.summary: Returns the smallest even integer that is greater than or equal to the specified valor.

ODD.summary: Rounds positive valor up the nearest odd number and negative valor down to the nearest odd number.

INT.summary: Returns the integer part of a number, rounding down toward negative infinity.

SQRT.summary: Returns the square root of a nonnegative number.

POWER.summary: Computes the specified base to the specified power.

EXP.summary: Computes Euler number (e) to the specified power.

LOG.summary: Computes the logarithm of the valor in provided base. The base padrãos to 10 if not specified.

MOD.summary: Returns the remainder after dividing the first argument by the second.

VALUE.summary: Converts the text string to a number.

CONCATENATE.summary: Joins together various valor tipos arguments into a single text valor.

FIND.summary: Finds an occurrence of stringToFind in whereToPesquisar string starting from an optional startFromPosition.(startFromPosition is 0 by padrão.) If no occurrence of stringToFind is found, the result will be 0.
FIND.example:
FIND("Teable", "Hello Teable") => 7
FIND("Teable", "Hello Teable", 5) => 7
FIND("Teable", "Hello Teable", 10) => 0

SEARCH.summary:
Searches for an occurrence of stringToFind in whereToPesquisar string starting from an optional startFromPosition. (startFromPosition is 0 by padrão.) If no occurrence of stringToFind is found, the result will be empty.
Similar to FIND(), though FIND() returns 0 rather than empty if no occurrence of stringToFind is found.
SEARCH.example:
SEARCH("Teable", "Hello Teable") => 7
SEARCH("Teable", "Hello Teable", 5) => 7
SEARCH("Teable", "Hello Teable", 10) => ""

MID.summary: Extract a substring of count characters starting at whereToStart.

LEFT.summary: Extract howMany characters from the beginning of the string.

RIGHT.summary: Extract howMany characters from the ending of the string.

REPLACE.summary:
Replaces the number of characters beginning with the start character with the replacement text.
(If you are looking for a way to find and replace all occurrences of old_text with novo_text, see SUBSTITUTE().)

REGEXP_REPLACE.summary: Replaces all substrings matching regular expression with replacement.

SUBSTITUTE.summary:
Replaces occurrences of old_text with novo_text.
You can optionally specify an index number (starting from 1) to replace just a specific occurrence of old_text. If no index number is specified, then all occurrences of old_text will be replaced.

TEXTBEFORE.summary: Returns the text before a delimiter.

TEXTSPLIT.summary: Splits text by a delimiter and returns multiple text valors.

REPT.summary: Repeats string by the specified number of times.

TRIM.summary: Removes whiteespaço at the beginning and end of string.

LEN.summary: Extract howMany characters from the beginning of the string.

T.summary: Returns the argument if it is text and blank otherwise.

ENCODE_URL_COMPONENT.summary: Replaces certain characters with encoded equivalents for use in constructing URLs or URIs. Does not encode the following characters: - _ . ~

IF.summary:
Returns valor1 if the logical argument is true, otherwise it returns valor2. Can also be used to make nested IF statements.
Can also be used to check if a cell is blank/is empty.

SWITCH.summary: Takes an expression, a list of possible valors for that expression, and for each one, a valor that the expression should take in that case. It can also take a padrão valor if the expression input does not match any of the defined patterns. In many cases, SWITCH() can be used instead of a nested IF() formula.

AND.summary: Returns true if all the arguments are true, returns false otherwise.

OR.summary: Returns true if any one of the arguments is true.

XOR.summary: Returns true if an odd number of arguments are true.

NOT.summary: Reverses the logical valor of its argument.

IS_ERROR.summary: Returns true if the expression causes an erro.

NOW.summary: Returns the atual date and time.

YEAR.summary: Returns the four-digit year of a datetime.

MONTH.summary: Returns the month of a datetime as a number between 1 (January) and 12 (December).

WEEKNUM.summary: Returns the week number in a year.

WEEKDAY.summary:
Returns the day of the week as an integer between 0 and 6, inclusive. You may optionally provide a second argument (either "Sunday" or "Monday") to start weeks on that day. If omitted, weeks start on Sunday by padrão. Example:
WEEKDAY(TODAY(), "Monday")

DAY.summary: Returns the day of the month of a datetime in the form of a number between 1-31.

HOUR.summary: Returns the hour of a datetime as a number between 0 (12:00am) and 23 (11:00pm).

MINUTE.summary: Returns the minute of a datetime as an integer between 0 and 59.

SECOND.summary: Returns the second of a datetime as an integer between 0 and 59.

FROMNOW.summary: Calculates the number of days between the atual date and another date.

TONOW.summary: Calculates the number of days between the atual date and another date.

DATETIME_DIFF.summary:
Returns the difference between datetimes in specified units. Padrão unit is "second".
Suportado units: "millisecond" (ms), "second" (s), "minute" (m), "hour" (h), "day" (d), "week" (w), "month" (M), "year" (y).
DATETIME_DIFF always subtracts the second argument from the first (`date1 - date2`). If you want elapsed time from a start date to a later end date, call `DATETIME_DIFF(endDate, startDate, unit)`.

WORKDAY.summary: Returns the workday to the start date, excluding the specified holidays

WORKDAY_DIFF.summary: Returns the number of working days after [startDate] up to and including [endDate]. This is a business-day count, not a calendar-day diff, so weekends and an optional list of holidays are excluded. For calendar-day elapsed time, use `DATETIME_DIFF(endDate, startDate, "day")`. The result is negative when [endDate] is earlier than [startDate], and WORKDAY_DIFF(startDate, WORKDAY(startDate, n)) returns n.

IS_SAME.summary: Compares two dates up to a unit and determines whether they are identical. Returns true if yes, false if no.

IS_AFTER.summary: Determines if date1 is later than date2. Returns true if yes, false if no.

IS_BEFORE.summary: Determines if date1 is earlier than date2. Returns true if yes, false if no.

DATE_ADD.summary: Adds specified "count" units to a datetime.

DATESTR.summary: Formats a datetime into a string (YYYY-MM-DD).

TIMESTR.summary: Formats a datetime into a time-only string (HH:mm:ss).

DATETIME_FORMAT.summary: Formats a datetime into a specified string. Suportado specifiers: YY, YYYY, M, MM, MMM, MMMM, D, DD, d, dd, ddd, dddd, H, HH, h, hh, m, mm, s, ss, SSS, Z, ZZ, A, a, LT, LTS, L, LL, LLL, LLLL, l, ll, lll, llll.

DATETIME_PARSE.summary: Interprets a text string as a structured date, with optional input format and locale parameters. The output format will always be formatted "M/D/YYYY h:mm a".

CREATED_TIME.summary: Returns the creation time of the atual registro.

LAST_MODIFIED_TIME.summary: Returns the date and time of the most recent modification made by a usuário in a non-computed campo in the tabela. Optionally pass a campo to track updates for that coluna only.

COUNTALL.summary: Returns the number of all elements including text and blanks.

COUNTA.summary: Returns the number of non-empty valors. This function counts both numeric and text valors.

COUNT.summary: Returns the number of numeric items.

ARRAY_JOIN.summary: Join the array of rollup items into a string with a separator.

ARRAY_UNIQUE.summary: Returns only unique items in the array.

ARRAY_FLATTEN.summary: Flattens the array by removing any array nesting. All items become elements of a single array.

ARRAY_COMPACT.summary: Removes empty strings and null valors from the array. Keeps "false" and strings that contain one or more blank characters.

RECORD_ID.summary: Returns the ID of the atual registro.

AUTO_NUMBER.summary: Returns the unique and incremented numbers for each registro.

FORMULA.summary: Fill in variables, operational characters, and functions to form formulas for calculations.
FORMULA.example:
Quoting the Coluna: {Campo nome}

Using operator: 100 * 2 + 300

Using function: SUM({Number Campo 1}, 100)

Using IF statement: 
IF(logical condition, "valor 1", "valor 2")
